title: Per-paper extraction
description: One paper's extraction. Each scored entry in `benchmarks` contributes candidate rows to refine. A key's absence from `benchmarks` means this extraction makes no claim about that benchmark — not that the paper lacks it.

arxiv_id.description: arxiv id, matches the paper at .cache/papers/{arxiv_id}/paper.md.

benchmarks.description: Map {benchmark_key → Scored | Absent}. Keys are benchmark ids from leaderboard/benchmarks/{key}.md frontmatter. A key is present only when this extraction has something to say about it — either an extractable score (Scored) or an explicit no-extractable-score determination (Absent). Unmentioned keys are omitted.

paper_hash.description: sha256: of paper.md. Injected at pack time; absent from the per-paper cache file.

extracted_at.description: Timestamp of the extraction run. Injected at pack time; absent from the per-paper cache file.

model_used.description: Model id that produced this extraction. Injected at pack time; absent from the per-paper cache file.

Absent.description: Paper mentions this benchmark but no extractable score (cited-only, unreadable, out-of-scope, figure-only).

reason.description: One-line reason the score could not be extracted.

ModelRow.description: One row in a results table.

name_in_paper.description: Method label from the paper's results table, verbatim. When the label is generic (Ours, Baseline, (b)), resolve to the method's canonical name from the paper's title/abstract/method section and emit that. Immutable after this stage.

label_quote.description: Exact label characters from the paper. Evidence only.

weight_type.description: shared = same checkpoint across benchmarks. finetuned = trained on this benchmark's data.

is_score_original.description: original: this paper ran the method AND introduces it. cited_baseline: number quoted from another source (populate cited_paper). reproduction: this paper re-ran a method it does not introduce. unknown: cannot tell.

model_paper.description: URL of the paper that introduces the method. Resolve from the paper's references. Any URL (arxiv, ACL, DOI, tech report). null only when the method has no public paper.

cited_paper.description: URL of the source this score is quoted from. Populate only when is_score_original='cited_baseline'. Any URL (arxiv, github, blog, tech report). null otherwise.

Scores.description: Required keys follow leaderboard/benchmarks/{bm}.md's Scoring section. All numeric values are in the benchmark's declared metric.range (benchmarks.json). Convert from paper's original scale when needed (typically 0-1 → 0-100 for % benchmarks).

overall_score.description: Paper's reported aggregate. null when absent or not locatable.

suite_scores.description: Sub-scores over benchmark-defined suites (e.g. LIBERO's 4 suites). Keys match the benchmark's declared suites.

task_scores.description: Per-task scores keyed by task name. Keys match the benchmark's declared tasks for standard protocols; non-standard protocols may include additional keys — keep them under the paper's verbatim names. Same shape as suite_scores.

quote.description: Exact characters from the paper. null is valid when the number was read from a figure/plot and no textual quote exists.

matches_standard.description: Whether the row's evaluation matches the benchmark's Standard in {bm}.md. If the rationale describes any Checks violation, use 'no' (not 'yes').

rationale.description: Why matches_standard has that value. Every factual claim (task count, demo count, split) must appear in evidence_quote.

evidence_quote.description: Verbatim paper text backing the rationale. A rationale claim with no matching quote downgrades matches_standard one step toward 'unknown'.
